00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "class.h"
00027 #include "classfile.h"
00028 #include "constantpool.h"
00029 #include "loader.h"
00030 #include "method.h"
00031 #include "memory.h"
00032 #include "native.h"
00033 #include "opcodes.h"
00034 #include "util.h"

00036 /******************************************************************************
00037  * Global declarations                                                        *
00038  ******************************************************************************/
00039 
00041 uint8_t halt_method_code[] = { WIDE, HALT, WIDE, HALT };
00042 
00045 exception_handler_t halt_exception_handler = {
00046     0, // start_pc
00047     4, // end_pc
00048     halt_method_code, // handler_pc
00049     NULL // catch_type
00050 };
00051 
00054 method_t halt_method = {
00055     "halt_method", // name
00056     NULL, // descriptor
00057     NULL, // cp
00058     0, // access_flags
00059     0, // args_size
00060     0, // index
00061     0, // max_stack
00062     0, // max_locals
00063     RET_VOID, // return_type
00064     4, // code_length
00065     1, // exception_table_length
00066     halt_method_code, // code
00067     { NULL } // handlers
00068 };
00069 
00071 static uint8_t abstract_method_code[] = { WIDE, METHOD_ABSTRACT };
00072 
00074 static uint8_t load_method_code[] = { WIDE, METHOD_LOAD };
00075 
00077 static uint8_t native_method_code[] = { WIDE, INVOKE_NATIVE };
00078 
00079 /******************************************************************************
00080  * Method implementation                                                      *
00081  ******************************************************************************/
00082 
00085 void init_dummy_methods( void )
00086 {
00087     class_t *object = bcl_resolve_class(NULL, "java/lang/Object");
00088 
00089     halt_exception_handler.catch_type = object;
00090     halt_method.cp = cp_create_dummy();
00091     halt_method.data.handlers = &halt_exception_handler;
00092 } // init_dummy_methods()
00093 
00098 method_manager_t *mm_create(uint32_t count)
00099 {
00100     method_manager_t *mm = gc_palloc(sizeof(method_manager_t));
00101 
00102 #ifndef NDEBUG
00103     mm->reserved = count;
00104 #endif // !NDEBUG
00105     mm->methods = gc_palloc(count * sizeof(method_t));
00106 
00107     return mm;
00108 } // mm_create()
00109 
00113 static void parse_method_descriptor(method_t *method)
00114 {
00115     const char *desc = method->descriptor;
00116     size_t i = 0, j = 0; // A couple of counters
00117     uint32_t args_size = 0; // Size (in stack slots) of the arguments
00118     uint32_t dimensions = 0; // Array dimensions
00119 
00120     switch (desc[i]) {
00121         case '(':
00122             i++;
00123             goto arguments_pass;
00124 
00125         default:
00126             goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00127     }
00128 
00129 arguments_pass:
00130 
00131     switch (desc[i]) {
00132         case ')':
00133             if ((dimensions != 0) || (desc[i + 1] == 0)) {
00134                 goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00135             }
00136 
00137             i++;
00138             goto return_type_pass;
00139 
00140         case 'B':
00141         case 'C':
00142         case 'S':
00143         case 'Z':
00144         case 'I':
00145         case 'F':
00146             dimensions = 0;
00147             args_size++;
00148             i++;
00149             goto arguments_pass;
00150 
00151         case 'J':
00152         case 'D':
00153             if (dimensions != 0) {
00154                 dimensions = 0;
00155                 args_size++;
00156             } else {
00157                 args_size += 2;
00158             }
00159 
00160             i++;
00161             goto arguments_pass;
00162 
00163         case 'L':
00164             dimensions = 0;
00165             j = i + 1;
00166 
00167             while (desc[j] != ';' && desc[j] != 0) {
00168                 j++;
00169             }
00170 
00171             if ((desc[j] == 0) || (j == i + 1)) {
00172                 goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00173             }
00174 
00175             args_size++;
00176             i = j + 1;
00177             goto arguments_pass;
00178 
00179         case '[':
00180             dimensions++;
00181             i++;
00182             goto arguments_pass;
00183 
00184         default:
00185             goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00186     }
00187 
00188 return_type_pass:
00189 
00190     switch (desc[i]) {
00191         case 'V':
00192             break;
00193 
00194         case 'B':
00195         case 'C':
00196         case 'F':
00197         case 'I':
00198         case 'S':
00199         case 'Z':
00200             break;
00201 
00202         case 'J':
00203         case 'D':
00204             break;
00205 
00206         case 'L':
00207             j = i + 1;
00208 
00209             while (desc[j] != ';' && desc[j] != 0) {
00210                 j++;
00211             }
00212 
00213             if ((desc[j] == 0) || (j == i + 1)) {
00214                 goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00215             }
00216 
00217             i = j;
00218             break;
00219 
00220         case '[':
00221              j = i;
00222 
00223             while (desc[j] == '[')
00224                 j++;
00225 
00226             if (desc[j] == 'L') {
00227                 while (desc[j] != ';' && desc[j] != 0) {
00228                     j++;
00229                 }
00230 
00231                 if ((desc[j] == 0) || (j == i + 1)) {
00232                     goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00233                 }
00234             } else {
00235                 switch (desc[j]) {
00236                     case 'B':
00237                     case 'C':
00238                     case 'D':
00239                     case 'F':
00240                     case 'I':
00241                     case 'J':
00242                     case 'S':
00243                     case 'Z':
00244                         break;
00245 
00246                     default:
00247                         goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00248                 }
00249             }
00250 
00251             i = j;
00252             break;
00253 
00254         default:
00255             goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00256     }
00257 
00258     if (desc[i + 1] != 0) {
00259         goto malformed_descriptor_exception;
00260     }
00261 
00262     if (!method_is_static(method)) {
00263         args_size++; // Include the 'this' pointer as an argument
00264     }
00265 
00266     if (args_size >= METHOD_ARGUMENTS_MAX) {
00267         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00268                 "Number of arguments in a method exceed the VM limits");
00269     }
00270 
00271     method->args_size = args_size;
00272     return;
00273 
00274 malformed_descriptor_exception:
00275     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR, "Malformed method descriptor");
00276 } // parse_method_descriptor()
00277 
00281 static void method_check_access_flags(method_t *method)
00282 {
00283     uint16_t access_flags;
00284 
00285     access_flags = method->access_flags;
00286 
00287     if (((access_flags & ACC_PRIVATE) && (access_flags & ACC_PROTECTED))
00288         || ((access_flags & ACC_PRIVATE) && (access_flags & ACC_PUBLIC))
00289         || ((access_flags & ACC_PROTECTED) && (access_flags & ACC_PUBLIC)))
00290     {
00291         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00292                 "Wrong combination of method access flags");
00293     }
00294 
00295     if ((access_flags & ACC_ABSTRACT)
00296         && ((access_flags & ACC_FINAL) || (access_flags & ACC_SYNCHRONIZED)
00297             || (access_flags & ACC_PRIVATE) || (access_flags & ACC_STATIC)
00298             || (access_flags & ACC_STRICT) || (access_flags & ACC_NATIVE)))
00299     {
00300         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00301                 "Wrong combination of method access flags");
00302     }
00303 
00304     if (method_is_init(method)) {
00305         if ((access_flags & ACC_STATIC) || (access_flags & ACC_FINAL)
00306             || (access_flags & ACC_SYNCHRONIZED) || (access_flags & ACC_NATIVE)
00307             || (access_flags & ACC_ABSTRACT))
00308         {
00309             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00310                     "Constructor is either static, final, synchronized, native "
00311                     "or abstract");
00312         }
00313     }
00314 } // method_check_access_flags()
00315 
00324 void mm_add(method_manager_t *mm, char *name, char *descriptor,
00325             uint16_t access_flags, const_pool_t *cp, method_attributes_t *attr)
00326 {
00327     uint32_t i;
00328     method_t *method;
00329 
00330     if (mm_get(mm, name, descriptor) != NULL) {
00331         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00332                 "Duplicated methods with same name and descriptor");
00333     }
00334 
00335     assert(mm->entries < mm->reserved);
00336 
00337     i = mm->entries;
00338     method = mm->methods + i;
00339 
00340     method->name = name;
00341     method->descriptor = descriptor;
00342     method->access_flags = access_flags;
00343     method->cp = cp;
00344     method_check_access_flags(method);
00345 
00346     if (access_flags & ACC_NATIVE) {
00347         if (attr->code_found == true) {
00348             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00349                     "Code attribute found for a method declared as native");
00350         }
00351 
00352         // A native method doesn't specify any of these
00353         method->max_stack = 0;
00354         method->max_locals = 0;
00355         method->code_length = 0;
00356         method->exception_table_length = 0;
00357         method->code = NULL;
00358     } else if (access_flags & ACC_ABSTRACT) {
00359         if (attr->code_found == true) {
00360             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00361                     "Code attribute found for a method declared as abstract");
00362         }
00363          // Make the method code point to the dummy abstract method code
00364         method->max_stack = 0;
00365         method->max_locals = 0;
00366         method->code_length = 1;
00367         method->exception_table_length = 0;
00368         method->code = abstract_method_code;
00369         method->data.handlers = NULL;
00370    } else {
00371         if (attr->code_found == false) {
00372             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00373                     "Java method lacks the Code attribute");
00374         }
00375 
00376         method->max_stack = attr->max_stack;
00377         method->max_locals = attr->max_locals;
00378         method->code_length = attr->code_length;
00379         method->exception_table_length = attr->exception_table_length;
00380         method->code = load_method_code;
00381         method->data.offset = attr->code_offset;
00382     }
00383 
00384     // Finally parse and validate the method descriptor
00385     parse_method_descriptor(method);
00386 
00387     mm->entries++;
00388 } // mm_add()
00389 
00397 method_t *mm_get(method_manager_t *mm, char *name, char *descriptor)
00398 {
00399     for (size_t i = 0; i < mm->entries; i++) {
00400         if (strcmp(mm->methods[i].name, name) == 0) {
00401             if (strcmp(mm->methods[i].descriptor, descriptor) == 0) {
00402                 return mm->methods + i;
00403             }
00404         }
00405     }
00406 
00407     return NULL;
00408 } // mm_get()
00409 
00414 uint32_t mm_get_count(method_manager_t *mm)
00415 {
00416     return mm->entries;
00417 } // mm_get_count()
00418 
00423 uint16_t method_create_packed_index(const method_t *method)
00424 {
00425     return (method->index << METHOD_ARGUMENTS_BITS) | method->args_size;
00426 } // method_create_packed_index()
00427 
00433 bool method_is_init(const method_t *method)
00434 {
00435     if (strcmp(method->name, "<init>") == 0) {
00436         return true;
00437     } else {
00438       return false;
00439     }
00440 } // method_is_init()
00441 
00447 uint32_t method_get_code_length(const method_t *method)
00448 {
00449     if (method_is_synchronized(method)) {
00450         return method->code_length + 1;
00451     } else {
00452         return method->code_length;
00453     }
00454 } // method_get_code_length()
00455 
00461 bool method_compare(const method_t *method1, const method_t *method2)
00462 {
00463     if ((strcmp(method1->name, method2->name) == 0)
00464         && (strcmp(method1->descriptor, method2->descriptor) == 0))
00465     {
00466         return true;
00467     } else {
00468         return false;
00469     }
00470 } // method_compare()
00471 
00476 void method_link_native(method_t *method, char *class_name)
00477 {
00478     const char *desc = method->descriptor;
00479 
00480     method->access_flags |= ACC_LINKED;
00481     method->code = native_method_code;
00482     method->data.function = native_method_lookup(class_name, method->name,
00483                                                  method->descriptor);
00484 
00485    if (method->data.function == NULL) {
00486         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00487                 "Native method implementation not found");
00488     }
00489 
00490     // Examine and set the return type
00491 
00492     while (*desc++ != ')')
00493         ;
00494 
00495     switch (*desc) {
00496         case 'V': method->return_type = RET_VOID;   break;
00497         case 'B': // FALLTHROUGH
00498         case 'C': // FALLTHROUGH
00499         case 'S': // FALLTHROUGH
00500         case 'Z': // FALLTHROUGH
00501         case 'I': method->return_type = RET_INT;    break;
00502         case 'J': method->return_type = RET_LONG;   break;
00503 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00504         case 'F': method->return_type = RET_FLOAT;  break;
00505         case 'D': method->return_type = RET_DOUBLE; break;
00506 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00507         case 'L': // FALLTHROUGH
00508         case '[': method->return_type = RET_OBJECT; break;
00509         default:  dbg_unreachable();
00510     }
00511  } // method_link_native()
00512 
00516 void method_purge(method_t *method)
00517 {
00518     gc_free(method->code);
00519     gc_free(method->data.handlers);
00520 } // method_purge()
00521 
00522
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:26 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
